0.name: 愚者
0.meaningUpright: New beginnings, spontaneity, faith in life, innocence
0.description: The Fool represents pure potential and the willingness to embark on a new journey with an open heart.
0.advice: Trust the process and step forward with courage.

1.name: 魔术师
1.meaningUpright: Will, manifestation, skill, creative power, resourcefulness
1.meaningReversed: Manipulation, blocked energy, trickery, false appearances
1.description: The Magician bridges the spiritual and material realms, transforming thought into reality.
1.advice: You have everything you need. Act decisively and with intention.

2.name: 女祭司
2.meaningUpright: Intuition, mystery, inner knowledge, stillness, the subconscious
2.meaningReversed: Secrets, ignoring intuition, disharmony, hidden agendas
2.description: The High Priestess invites you into the world of the subconscious and deep inner wisdom.
2.advice: Listen to your inner wisdom. Silence holds the answers you seek.

3.name: 女皇
3.description: The Empress embodies divine femininity, life force, and the fertile power of nature.
3.advice: Nurture yourself and others. Abundance is right before you.

4.name: 皇帝
4.meaningReversed: Domination, rigidity, lack of flexibility, control issues
4.description: The Emperor provides solid foundations and a stable structure for your actions.
4.advice: Build a strong foundation. Act with authority and responsibility.

5.name: 教皇
5.description: The Hierophant bridges the sacred and the mundane as teacher and guide.
5.advice: Seek the wisdom of teachers or your own spiritual path.

6.name: 恋人
6.meaningUpright: Love, harmony, values, heart choices, relationships
6.meaningReversed: Separation, indecision, conflict of values, misalignment
6.description: The Lovers symbolise union, harmony, and important life choices made from the heart.
6.advice: Make a decision aligned with your deepest values and heart's truth.

7.name: 战车
7.meaningReversed: Aggression, lack of control, scattered energy, defeat
7.description: The Chariot is the triumph of will over obstacles through disciplined action.
7.advice: Hold the reins firmly. Your determination will carry you to your goal.

8.name: 力量
8.meaningUpright: Courage, patience, compassion, inner strength, confidence
8.meaningReversed: Weakness, self-doubt, lack of faith, insecurity
8.description: Strength is the gentle power rooted in love and spiritual courage.
8.advice: Approach the situation with love and calm rather than force.

9.name: 隐士
9.meaningUpright: Introspection, reflection, wisdom, solitude, inner guidance
9.meaningReversed: Isolation, loneliness, withdrawal, cutting off from the world
9.description: The Hermit illuminates the path to inner truth through silence and contemplation.
9.advice: Step back for a while and seek answers within yourself.

10.name: 命运之轮
10.meaningUpright: Change, life cycles, karma, fate, turning point
10.meaningReversed: Bad luck, resistance to change, stagnation, breaking cycles
10.description: The Wheel of Fortune reminds us of the constant movement and cyclical nature of all existence.
10.advice: Accept change as part of the cycle. What ends makes way for the new.

11.name: 正义
11.meaningUpright: Truth, balance, fairness, karma, cause and effect
11.description: Justice symbolises objective truth and the consequences of our actions.
11.advice: Be honest with yourself and others. Truth always comes to light.

12.name: 倒吊人
12.meaningUpright: Surrender, new perspective, sacrifice, pause, letting go
12.meaningReversed: Resistance to surrender, stagnation, martyrdom, delays
12.description: The Hanged Man pauses us to see the world from a fresh, inverted angle.
12.advice: Release control. Suspension will bring you a new perspective and wisdom.

13.name: 死神
13.meaningReversed: Resistance to change, stagnation, fear of endings
13.description: The Death card does not signify physical death but deep transformation and new beginnings.
13.advice: Allow the old to pass. Transformation opens the door to new life.

14.name: 节制
14.description: Temperance teaches us the art of balancing and finding the golden mean.
14.advice: Find harmony between extremes. Patience is your strength.

15.name: 恶魔
15.meaningUpright: Addiction, materialism, illusion, bondage, shadow self
15.meaningReversed: Liberation, release, breaking chains, reclaiming power
15.description: The Devil embodies our addictions and the limitations we impose on ourselves.
15.advice: Recognise your bonds and begin the process of liberation. You have the power to break free.

16.name: 塔
16.meaningUpright: Chaos, sudden change, awakening, revelation, upheaval
16.meaningReversed: Avoiding disaster, fear of change, delaying the inevitable
16.description: The Tower shatters old structures to make room for authenticity and truth.
16.advice: Though the change is abrupt, this clearing paves the way for a true foundation.

17.name: 星星
17.meaningReversed: Loss of faith, pessimism, hopelessness, discouragement
17.description: The Star brings healing, hope, and renewal after difficult experiences.
17.advice: Have faith. After darkness, starlight always comes.

18.name: 月亮
18.meaningUpright: Illusion, fear, the subconscious, dreams, uncertainty
18.meaningReversed: Clarity, dispelling confusion, emerging from darkness
18.description: The Moon illuminates our fears and the shadowy corners of the subconscious.
18.advice: Face your own fears. Not everything is as it seems.

19.name: 太阳
19.meaningUpright: Joy, success, vitality, clarity, positivity, radiance
19.meaningReversed: Naivety, blocked joy, excessive optimism, temporary setbacks
19.description: The Sun brings clarity, vitality, and genuine happiness in every area of life.
19.advice: Shine your full light. This is a time of joy and success.

20.name: 审判
20.meaningReversed: Self-criticism, doubt, fear of judgement, ignoring the call
20.description: Judgement symbolises deep awakening and the answer to a spiritual calling.
20.advice: Answer your higher calling. It is time for renewal and a new chapter.

21.name: 世界
21.meaningReversed: Incompleteness, lack of closure, procrastination, stagnation
21.description: The World signifies the fullness of experience, completion of a cycle, and celebration of achievement.
21.advice: Celebrate your accomplishments. One cycle ends; a new one begins.

c1.name: 圣杯A
c1.meaningUpright: New emotions, love, intuition, emotional abundance, beginnings
c1.meaningReversed: Blocked emotions, repressed feelings, loss of connection
c1.description: The Ace of Cups symbolises pure love and emotional new beginnings.
c1.advice: Open your heart to love and new emotional possibilities.

c2.name: 圣杯二
c2.description: The Two of Cups represents the depth of connection and mutual feeling.
c2.advice: Nurture your relationships and mutual understanding.

c3.name: 圣杯三
c3.meaningReversed: Excess, gossip, isolation from friends, over-indulgence
c3.description: The Three of Cups is a celebration with loved ones and the joy of community.
c3.advice: Celebrate together with others. Shared moments of joy hold great power.

c4.name: 圣杯四
c4.meaningReversed: New opportunities, new awakening, motivation, openness
c4.description: The Four of Cups invites inner reflection and re-evaluation of desires.
c4.advice: Pause and consider what you truly want. A new opportunity may be right before you.

c5.name: 圣杯五
c5.meaningReversed: Acceptance, forgiveness, new perspective, moving on
c5.description: The Five of Cups teaches us that after loss something always remains on which we can build.
c5.advice: Allow yourself to grieve, but do not forget what you still have.

c6.name: 圣杯六
c6.meaningUpright: Nostalgia, memories, innocence, the past, simplicity
c6.meaningReversed: Stuck in the past, naivety, living in fantasy
c6.description: The Six of Cups evokes the beauty of memories and the simplicity of former days.
c6.advice: Draw joy from memories, but remember that life moves forward.

c7.name: 圣杯七
c7.meaningUpright: Fantasy, illusion, many choices, wishful thinking, dreams
c7.meaningReversed: Clarity, realism, making the right choice, focus
c7.description: The Seven of Cups reveals an abundance of possibilities, though not all are real.
c7.advice: Distinguish dream from reality. Focus on what is truly attainable.

c8.name: 圣杯八
c8.meaningUpright: Walking away, seeking deeper meaning, withdrawal, letting go
c8.meaningReversed: Fear of abandonment, avoiding truth, staying in the wrong situation
c8.description: The Eight of Cups symbolises the courage to leave behind what does not fulfil us.
c8.advice: Have the courage to walk away from what does not serve your spiritual growth.

c9.name: 圣杯九
c9.meaningReversed: Materialism, wishful thinking, illusion of happiness, greed
c9.description: The Nine of Cups is the card of emotional fulfilment and life satisfaction.
c9.advice: Your wishes can come true. Be grateful for what you already have.

c10.name: 圣杯十
c10.meaningUpright: Family, harmony, happiness, fulfilment, long-term joy
c10.meaningReversed: Family conflicts, lack of harmony, broken home, loss of meaning
c10.description: The Ten of Cups symbolises ideal family happiness and emotional completeness.
c10.advice: Nurture family bonds and close relationships. That is your true treasure.

c11.name: 圣杯侍从
c11.meaningUpright: Creativity, intuition, sensitivity, new feelings, imagination
c11.description: The Page of Cups is an artistic soul full of imagination and emotional sensitivity.
c11.advice: Trust your intuition and give expression to the creativity you carry in your heart.

c12.name: 圣杯骑士
c12.meaningUpright: Romance, charm, imagination, following dreams, idealism
c12.description: The Knight of Cups moves forward with a heart full of dreams and romantic ideals.
c12.advice: Follow your dreams, but do not lose yourself in illusions. Stay true to yourself.

c13.name: 圣杯王后
c13.meaningUpright: Empathy, intuition, nurturing, emotional wisdom, compassion
c13.description: The Queen of Cups embodies the depth of emotion and loving wisdom of the heart.
c13.advice: Use your intuition and empathy as a guide in your relationships.

c14.name: 圣杯国王
c14.meaningUpright: Emotional maturity, diplomacy, wisdom, calm, balance
c14.meaningReversed: Emotional manipulation, repression of feelings, instability
c14.description: The King of Cups rules over emotions with wisdom and stability.
c14.advice: Be master of your emotions, not their victim. Act with balance and wisdom.

w1.name: 权杖A
w1.meaningUpright: New beginnings, inspiration, potential, creative energy, spark
w1.meaningReversed: Delays, lack of motivation, creative block, missed opportunity
w1.description: The Ace of Wands is the spark that ignites the flame of new ventures and passion.
w1.advice: Act on this creative energy. Now is the time to begin.

w2.name: 权杖二
w2.meaningUpright: Planning, vision, partnership, future, bold steps
w2.meaningReversed: Fear of the unknown, lack of planning, playing it safe
w2.description: The Two of Wands invites bold planning and ambitious steps forward.
w2.advice: Think long-term and boldly plan your next move.

w3.name: 权杖三
w3.meaningReversed: Obstacles, delays, frustration, lack of foresight
w3.description: The Three of Wands symbolises the moment of observing the fruits of your work and planning expansion.
w3.advice: Your work is bearing results. It is time to broaden your horizons.

w4.name: 权杖四
w4.meaningReversed: Lack of harmony, overload, postponing celebration, conflict at home
w4.description: The Four of Wands is a joyful celebration of achievements and a sense of belonging.
w4.advice: Celebrate your successes. You have earned a moment of joy and gratitude.

w5.name: 权杖五
w5.meaningReversed: Avoiding conflict, end of struggle, harmony, resolution
w5.description: The Five of Wands reveals the dynamic energy of competition and creative chaos.
w5.advice: Approach challenges with an open mind. Conflict can lead to growth.

w6.name: 权杖六
w6.meaningUpright: Triumph, victory, recognition, success, public acknowledgement
w6.meaningReversed: Arrogance, downfall, uncertainty of success, egotism
w6.description: The Six of Wands celebrates public recognition and longed-for victory.
w6.advice: Your success deserves recognition. Accept it with grace and humility.

w7.name: 权杖七
w7.meaningUpright: Perseverance, defence, challenge, determination, standing firm
w7.description: The Seven of Wands demands perseverance in the face of challenges and defending your position.
w7.advice: Stand firmly by your convictions. Perseverance is your strength.

w8.name: 权杖八
w8.meaningUpright: Swift action, movement, news, progress, momentum
w8.meaningReversed: Delays, frustration, information chaos, lack of direction
w8.description: The Eight of Wands brings rapid changes and an acceleration of events.
w8.advice: Act quickly when the opportunity arises. Timing matters.

w9.name: 权杖九
w9.meaningUpright: Perseverance, resilience, boundaries, strength, near completion
w9.meaningReversed: Exhaustion, lack of resilience, excessive caution, burnout
w9.description: The Nine of Wands symbolises the strength gained through difficult experiences.
w9.advice: You are stronger than you think. One last effort and the goal will be reached.

w10.name: 权杖十
w10.meaningUpright: Burden, overwhelm, duty, responsibility, carrying too much
w10.description: The Ten of Wands reveals the weight of excessive obligations and the need to delegate.
w10.advice: You do not have to carry everything alone. Share part of the burden with others.

w11.name: 权杖侍从
w11.meaningUpright: Enthusiasm, exploration, ambition, energy, free spirit
w11.meaningReversed: Lack of direction, inconsistency, immaturity, scattered energy
w11.description: The Page of Wands is an energetic adventurer ready for new challenges.
w11.advice: Be bold and enthusiastic. Your energy is contagious.

w12.name: 权杖骑士
w12.meaningReversed: Scattered energy, lack of plan, reckless decisions, haste
w12.description: The Knight of Wands charges forward with unbridled passion and courage.
w12.advice: Channel your energy into concrete actions so as not to fall into chaos.

w13.name: 权杖王后
w13.description: The Queen of Wands embodies powerful femininity, independence, and magnetic charisma.
w13.advice: Be yourself without apology. Your authenticity is your greatest strength.

w14.name: 权杖国王
w14.description: The King of Wands is a visionary leader who inspires others with passion and determination.
w14.advice: Lead with vision and passion. Your leadership can inspire others.

s1.name: 宝剑A
s1.meaningUpright: Mental clarity, truth, breakthrough, new thinking, justice
s1.meaningReversed: Confusion of thought, disinformation, brutality, chaos
s1.description: The Ace of Swords is the sharpness of intellect and the power of truth that cuts through illusions.
s1.advice: Seek truth and speak with clear, precise intention.

s2.name: 宝剑二
s2.description: The Two of Swords symbolises a difficult choice and the necessity of looking beyond appearances.
s2.advice: Remove the blindfold. The true answer lies within you.

s3.name: 宝剑三
s3.meaningReversed: Healing, forgiveness, release from pain, recovery
s3.description: The Three of Swords reveals the pain of the heart, a necessary stage on the path to healing.
s3.advice: Allow yourself to feel the pain. Healing begins with accepting the truth.

s4.name: 宝剑四
s4.meaningReversed: Return to action, anxiety, lack of rest, burnout
s4.description: The Four of Swords encourages pausing and recuperating after intense effort.
s4.advice: Allow yourself to rest. Peace of mind will restore your strength.

s5.name: 宝剑五
s5.meaningUpright: Conflict, defeat, dishonesty, hostility, hollow victory
s5.meaningReversed: Reconciliation, resolution, moving past conflict, forgiveness
s5.description: The Five of Swords reveals the consequences of battle and the need for wisdom in choosing your fights.
s5.advice: Not every battle is worth winning. Wisdom lies in choosing your fights.

s6.name: 宝剑六
s6.meaningUpright: Transition, journey, moving toward calm, rite of passage
s6.meaningReversed: Resistance to change, stuck in problems, ongoing turbulence
s6.description: The Six of Swords symbolises a slow emergence from difficulty toward calmer waters.
s6.advice: Move forward. Better times await on the other side.

s7.name: 宝剑七
s7.meaningUpright: Deception, strategy, escape, cunning, acting alone
s7.meaningReversed: Return to honesty, exposure of deception, confession
s7.description: The Seven of Swords points to the need for a considered strategy or warns against deception.
s7.advice: Be wary of dishonesty — your own or others'. Strategy requires integrity.

s8.name: 宝剑八
s8.meaningReversed: Liberation, new perspective, breaking limitations, empowerment
s8.description: The Eight of Swords reveals restrictions that we often impose upon ourselves in the mind.
s8.advice: Your bonds are less real than you think. You have the power to free yourself.

s9.name: 宝剑九
s9.meaningUpright: Fear, anxiety, nightmares, worry, mental anguish
s9.meaningReversed: Hope, recovery from fear, seeking help, relief
s9.description: The Nine of Swords symbolises the suffering of the mind and nocturnal anxieties.
s9.advice: You are not alone in your fear. Reach out for support and share your burden.

s10.name: 宝剑十
s10.meaningUpright: Ending, pain, defeat, but also a new dawn, rock bottom
s10.meaningReversed: Unwanted change, resistance, clinging to pain, inability to let go
s10.description: The Ten of Swords marks the end of a cycle which, though painful, opens the door to rebirth.
s10.advice: What has ended has ended. Breathe — a new dawn is coming.

s11.name: 宝剑侍从
s11.meaningReversed: Gossip, superficiality, dishonesty, all talk no action
s11.description: The Page of Swords is a sharp, inquisitive intellect hungry for knowledge and truth.
s11.advice: Ask, investigate, question. Your mind is your greatest weapon.

s12.name: 宝剑骑士
s12.meaningUpright: Ambition, swift action, determination, intellect, directness
s12.meaningReversed: Impulsiveness, lack of empathy, arrogance, recklessness
s12.description: The Knight of Swords charges at a goal with the full force of intellect and determined will.
s12.advice: Act decisively, but remember the impact on others.

s13.name: 宝剑王后
s13.meaningUpright: Independence, clarity of thought, boundaries, perception, truth
s13.meaningReversed: Harshness, emotional coldness, bitter experience, cruelty
s13.description: The Queen of Swords combines intellectual sharpness with wisdom gained through life experience.
s13.advice: Be clear and direct. Your strength flows from truth and healthy boundaries.

s14.name: 宝剑国王
s14.meaningUpright: Intellectual power, authority, clarity, justice, truth
s14.meaningReversed: Tyranny, manipulation, lack of empathy, abuse of power
s14.description: The King of Swords rules through the power of intellect and speaks truth without compromise.
s14.advice: Act with intellectual authority and clarity. Truth is your strength.

p1.name: 星币A
p1.meaningUpright: Abundance, new financial opportunity, manifestation, prosperity
p1.meaningReversed: Lost opportunity, greed, lack of stability, poor planning
p1.description: The Ace of Pentacles symbolises the seed of material prosperity and new opportunities.
p1.advice: Seize the new financial or material opportunity. The ground beneath your feet is solid.

p2.name: 星币二
p2.meaningUpright: Balance, adaptability, resource management, juggling priorities
p2.description: The Two of Pentacles reveals the art of juggling resources while maintaining balance.
p2.advice: Be flexible and balance your commitments with wisdom.

p3.name: 星币三
p3.meaningUpright: Craftsmanship, collaboration, learning, quality of work, teamwork
p3.meaningReversed: Lack of harmony at work, mediocrity, conflict, poor planning
p3.description: The Three of Pentacles celebrates excellence in craft and effective collaboration.
p3.advice: Invest in your skills and collaborate with others for better results.

p4.name: 星币四
p4.meaningReversed: Miserliness, greed, fear of loss, hoarding
p4.description: The Four of Pentacles symbolises the need for financial security but also the risk of excessive control.
p4.advice: Enjoy your security, but do not let fear of loss limit your life.

p5.name: 星币五
p5.meaningUpright: Financial hardship, poverty, isolation, difficulty, hardship
p5.meaningReversed: Return to stability, help arriving, healing, recovery
p5.description: The Five of Pentacles reveals material and emotional hardship, yet help is available nearby.
p5.advice: Do not be ashamed to ask for help. Support is available — you only need to accept it.

p6.name: 星币六
p6.meaningUpright: Generosity, giving, sharing, balance of exchange
p6.meaningReversed: Debts, inequality, conditional giving, strings attached
p6.description: The Six of Pentacles teaches that giving and receiving must be in harmony.
p6.advice: Give generously where you can, but also attend to your own needs.

p7.name: 星币七
p7.meaningUpright: Patience, assessment, long-term planning, investment, reward
p7.meaningReversed: Lack of reward, discouragement, impatience, poor returns
p7.description: The Seven of Pentacles encourages assessing the work done so far and patient anticipation of results.
p7.advice: Your work is bearing fruit, though slowly. Have patience.

p8.name: 星币八
p8.meaningReversed: Lack of ambition, perfectionism blocking action, mediocrity
p8.description: The Eight of Pentacles is a dedication to mastering a craft through persistent work.
p8.advice: Be diligent and focused on honing your skills.

p9.name: 星币九
p9.description: The Nine of Pentacles symbolises well-earned prosperity and independence.
p9.advice: Enjoy the fruits of your labour. You have taken care of yourself and it shows.

p10.name: 星币十
p10.meaningUpright: Wealth, legacy, family, long-term success, inheritance
p10.meaningReversed: Family conflicts, loss of legacy, short-sightedness, instability
p10.description: The Ten of Pentacles represents the fullness of material prosperity and lasting family happiness.
p10.advice: Think about the legacy you are leaving. Build on enduring foundations.

p11.name: 星币侍从
p11.meaningReversed: Lack of ambition, daydreaming without action, missed opportunity
p11.description: The Page of Pentacles is a persistent student who sets goals and works toward them systematically.
p11.advice: Learn and work consistently. Your perseverance will bear fruit.

p12.name: 星币骑士
p12.meaningReversed: Stagnation, lack of flexibility, excessive caution, stubbornness
p12.description: The Knight of Pentacles moves slowly but surely, focused on the goal.
p12.advice: Be perseverant and reliable. Slow, systematic action leads to success.

p13.name: 星币王后
p13.description: The Queen of Pentacles combines warmth with practical wisdom and the ability to create abundance.
p13.advice: Care for others with tenderness, but remember your own needs too.

p14.name: 星币国王
p14.meaningUpright: Wealth, leadership, security, success, wise management
p14.meaningReversed: Greed, corruption, materialism at the expense of relationships, stubbornness
p14.description: The King of Pentacles is the embodiment of material success, stability, and wise stewardship.
p14.advice: Manage your resources with wisdom and generosity. True success serves others.
